CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Off Calendar Sep-17-2021 Continued To Dec-08-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Abj Construction, Inc. And J.U.V. Investments, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 585
Off Calendar Aug-24-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Seamus Collins Construction, Inc., a purported California corporation, and Seamus Collins, an individual.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Gergory Gerson, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs ABJ Construction, Inc. a California corporation, and J.U.V. Investments, LLC, a California limited liability company.*
Mr. Gerson appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Seamus Collins Construction, Inc., a purported California corporation, or Seamus Collins, an individual.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract, Money Had & Received, Recovery of License Board and Breachof Promissory Note was filed on November 1, 2019, and personally served on defendant WESCO Insurance Company's Authorized Agent of Process, and defendant Seamus Collins, on November 4, 2019 and November 6, 2019, respectively.
Cross-Complaint by WESCO Insurance Comapny was filed on December 23, 2019.
Default as to defendants Seamus Collins Construction, Inc., a purported California corporation, and Seamus Collins, an individual, was entered on December 9, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed April 27, 2020.
Defendant/cross-complainant WESCO Insurance Company's cross-complaint dismissed March 2, 2021.
Dismissal as to defendant WESCO Insurance Company filed March 19, 2021.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs ABJ Construction, Inc. a California corporation, and J.U.V. Investments, LLC, a California limited liability company, and against the defaulted defendants Seamus Collins Construction, Inc., a purported California corporation, and Seamus Collins, an individual, as follows:

JUDGMENT FOR PLAINTIFF ABJ CONSTRUCTION AGAINST DEFENDANT SEAMUS COLLINS CONSTRUCTION:
Damages/Principal - $14,087.33.
Prejudgment Interest - $2,323.64.
Costs - $319.13.
Total - $16,730.10.

JUDGMENT FOR PLAINTIF J.U.V. INVESTMENTS AGAINST SEAMUS COLLLINS CONSTRUCTION:
Damages/Principal - $61,946.12
Prejudgment Interest - $10,657.16.
Costs - $539.71.
Total - $73,142.99.

JUDGMENT FOR PLAINTIFF J.U.V. INVESTMENTS AGAINST DEFENDANT SEAMUS COLLINS:
Damages/Principal - $25,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $4,301.80.
Attorney Fees - $13,696.49.
Costs - $558.84.
Total - $43,557.13.

Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

*On July 20, 2021, the Court heard testimony from Vladimir Chernyavskiy, CFO and Managing Member of J.U.V. Investments, LLC, and Leon Kemel, Project Manager for ABJ Construction, Inc. At that time, the Court waived the appearance of Messrs. Chernyavskiy and Kemel at today's hearing.

Notice Of Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs ABJ CONSTRUCTION, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, J.U.V. INVESTMENTS, LLC's Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions:

NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO PLAINTIFF ABJ CONSTRUCCTION INC.'S MOTION TO HAVE ITS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION SERVED ON DEFENDANT SEAMUS COLLINS CONSTRUCTION INC AND SEAMUS COLLIS AN INDIVIDUAL (COLLECTIVELY "COLLINS" OR "DEFENDANT") DEEMED ADMITTED. ACCORDINGLY IT IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:
(1) DEFENDANT HAS TEN (10) DAY FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER TO PROVIDE FULL AND COMPLETE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR ADMISSION NOS.1 - 13.
(2) IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH PARAGRAPH (1), ALL REQUESTS WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT WIL BE PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE WHICH WOULD CONTRADICT SAID ADMISSION.
(3) MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINT DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500.00 PAYMENT OF WHICH SHOULD BE MADE TO PLAINTIFF AND ITS COUNSEL WITHIN THE (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
